Motion For Sanctions Per Code Of Civil Procedure Sec 128.7
Off Calendar Jun-20-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Defendant Verna Shaheen's motion for CCP 128.7 sanctions.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Verna Shaheen's motion for CCP 128.7 sanctions is denied. First, the motion is belated, as defendant answered the complained-of complaint more than a year ago. Second, before a summary adjudication motion, California courts accept a complaint's allegations as true no matter how unlikely or improbable. (Del E. Webb Corp. v. Structural Materials Corp. (1981) 123 Cal.App.3d 593, 604.) Third, plaintiff's arguments are nonfrivolous and warranted by the evidence and law. (See, e.g., Opp. 7:17-9:15.) If defendant believed this case belonged in the probate department, she should have sought a transfer. No sanctions are awarded to either side.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B, Ulmer Jr., Clerk: R. Woods, Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Defendant Verna Shaheen'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Under Seal
Off Calendar May-24-2023 Continued To Jun-20-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

MOTION TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE WITH SUBPOENAS
Off Calendar Jan-31-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No Appearances in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF GREGORY GLASSMAN'S MOTION TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE WITH SUBPOENAS

PLAINTIFF, GREGORY GLASSMAN'S UNOPPOSED MOTION FOR AN ORDER COMPELLING THIRD PARTY, SAN FRANCISCO ADULT PROTECTION SERVICES ("APS) TO COMPLY WITH SUBPOENAS FOR PRODUCTION OF BUUSINESS RECORDS AND PERSONAL APPEARANCE IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS:

(1) WITHIN FIFTEEN (15) CALENDAR DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER, ADULT PROTECTION SERVICES WILL PRODUCE TO PLAINTIFF DOCUMENTS RESPONSIVE TO THE DEPOSITION SUBPOENAS PREVIOUSLY SERVED UPON IT AS TO HARRY SAMUEL GLASSMAN AND PEGGY BARTEL. (COPIES OF SUBPOENA ARE ATTACHED AS EXHIBITS 1 & 2 TO THIS ORDER)

(2) IF APS SO DESIRES IT IS AUTHORIZED TO REDACT FROM THE RECORDS THE IDENTITY OF THE INDIVIDUAL(S) WHO REPORTED THE LIVING CONDITIONS UNDER WHICH HARRY GLASSMAN AND PEFGGY BARTELS WERE ALLEGEDLY LIVING AT THE TIME THEY WERE BROUGHT TO THE ATTENTION OF APS. ALSO, APS SHALL MARK ALL RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS AS "CONFIDENTIAL".

(3) THE COURT WILL IMPOSE NO MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST ANY PARTY AS NONE WERE SOUGHT.
COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF SHOULD PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER CONSISTENT WITH THIS TENTATIVE RULING, SUBMIIT A COPY TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER A COPY IN WORD FORM TO THE COURT (rennepar85@gmaill.com).
